reasoning: The document contains exactly 5 major defects, which places it at the threshold for HUMAN_REVIEW according to the count-based routing rules. The major issues involve image extraction anomalies (both reported missing figures and an excessive overall image count ratio), tables split across pages with duplicated headers, displaced hyperlinks, and table header markup issues.

concerns.analysis: [Downgraded: image_count_ratio=1.77, placeholder_ratio=0.00 contradict LLM claim] Several meaningful data visualizations and charts present in the source PDF (Figures 14-18 and Figures 21-23) are missing from the HTML output. Instead of rendering as `` tags, they have been replaced by text placeholders (e.g., `[Image: ...]`). Because these are complex line charts representing Spawning Stock Biomass (SSB) projections and cumulative landings, the user loses critical information that cannot be fully conveyed by the brief placeholder text alone.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Source PDF pages 56-58 and 61-62 contain visual line graphs for Figures 14 through 18, and Figures 21 through 23.
concerns.output_quote:
The HTML output lacks tags for these figures and instead displays plain text paragraphs such as: "

[Image: Line chart showing projections of Spawning Stock Biomass (SSB) from 2018 to 2050 under different fishing scenarios...]

"

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 2 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=2. Examples: pages 47-48: 5 cols (issue, ncdmf, southern flounder advisory committee...); pages 48-49: 5 cols (issue, ncdmf, southern flounder advisory committee...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 1.8x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=1.77. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 6 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=6, examples=['https://www.implan.com', 'http://www.implan.com/', 'mailto:Michael.loeffler@ncdenr.gov']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Year"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

On page 27, the source document contains the underlined and italicized text 'Advisory Committee Recommendations'. The pipeline extracted this line as a regular paragraph rather than a heading and applied markdown italics (*Advisory Committee Recommendations*). Because it is not designated as a heading and markdown does not support underlines, the specific underline emphasis is lost.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: "Advisory Committee Recommendations (Refer to Table 13 for a comparison of recommendations)" (where 'Advisory Committee Recommendations' is underlined and italicized).
concerns.output_quote: "*Advisory Committee Recommendations* (Refer to Table 13 for a comparison of recommendations)"

concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=61. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.

action_items: Check Figures 14-18 and 21-23 to ensure charts and data visualizations are present and properly rendered.
Investigate the high image count ratio to remove any duplicated or improperly segmented images.
Merge the 2 tables that were split across page breaks and remove the duplicated header rows.
Move the 6 standalone hyperlinks inline within their appropriate paragraphs.
Fix the table header markup where data rows are incorrectly tagged as headers.
